description_long: This object consists of two paper envelopes, one of which is sealed with a prominent red wax seal on the flap, while the other is open and unfolded, revealing its interior. The sealed envelope has a classic rectangular shape with a triangular flap, and the wax seal is centrally placed, adding a touch of formality and security. The open envelope is displayed with its flap and body fully unfolded, showing the creases and folds typical of paper envelopes. Both envelopes are a light gray color, with smooth, matte surfaces and no visible text or markings. The wax seal stands out as the only colored element, providing a visual contrast to the otherwise monochromatic appearance. There are no visible address markings, postage stamps, or postal service logos on either envelope.
description: Two light gray paper envelopes, one sealed with a red wax seal and the other open and unfolded, both with smooth, matte surfaces and no visible markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the sealed envelope with a red wax seal and the open envelope lying flat, both in light gray.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the envelopes and the unfolded, zigzag shape of the open envelope.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back sides of both envelopes, with the open envelope's flap partially visible.
description_view_3: Top-down view emphasizing the unfolded, angular shape of the open envelope and the thinness of the sealed envelope.
